2605.03244.title: S^2tory: Story Spine Distillation for Movie Script Summarization
2605.03244.paper_summary: Movie scripts pose a fundamental challenge for automatic summarization due to their non-linear, cross-cut narrative structure, which makes surface-level saliency methods ineffective at preserving core story progression. To address this, we introduce S^2tory (Story Spine Distillation), a narratology-grounded framework that leverages character development trajectories to identify plot nuclei, the essential events that drive the narrative forward, while filtering out peripheral satellite events that merely enrich atmosphere or emotion. Our Narrative Expert Agent (NEAgent) performs theory-constrained reasoning, whose distilled knowledge conditions a small model to identify plot nuclei. Another model then uses these plot nuclei to generate the summary. Experiments on the MovieSum dataset demonstrate state-of-the-art semantic fidelity at approximately 3.5x compression, and zero-shot evaluation on BookSum confirms strong out-of-domain generalization. Human evaluation further validates that narratological theory provides an indispensable foundation for modeling complex, non-linear narratives.
2605.03244.paper_summary_zh: 電影劇本因其非線性、交錯的敘事結構，對於自動摘要提出了根本挑戰，這使得表面層的顯著性方法無法有效地保留核心故事的進展。為了解決這個問題，我們引入了 S^2tory（故事脊樑蒸餾），這是一個基於敘事學的框架，利用角色發展軌跡來識別情節核，即推動敘事前進的基本事件，同時過濾掉僅僅豐富氛圍或情感的周邊衛星事件。我們的敘事專家代理（NEAgent）執行理論約束推理，其蒸餾的知識使小模型能夠識別情節核。然後，另一個模型使用這些情節核來生成摘要。在 MovieSum 數據集上的實驗顯示出約 3.5 倍壓縮下的最先進語義保真度，而在 BookSum 上的零樣本評估則確認了強大的跨領域泛化能力。人類評估進一步驗證了敘事學理論為建模複雜的非線性敘事提供了不可或缺的基礎。
2605.03244.author: Mingzhe Lu et.al.
2605.03244.authors: Mingzhe Lu, Yanbing Liu, Qihao Wang, Jiarui Zhang, Jiayue Wu, Yue Hu, Yunpeng Li, Yangyan Xu
